summary: Of the 13 terms, 11 receive STAYS verdicts and 2 receive BOUNDARY verdicts. No terms are transferred out. The cluster as constituted is broadly coherent around the characteristic of divine redemptive action received by the human person — salvation, atonement, and gift-in-salvation-context all share the structural feature of God acting to rescue/cover/give, and the human inner being standing at the receiving end (guilt resolved, wrath averted, soul rescued, gratitude arising). The three semantic registers are genuinely inter-dependent: atonement is the mechanism that grounds eschatological rescue; gift-language names the mode of reception; salvation-language names the outcome. They cannot be cleanly separated at the verse level without fracturing the theological unity the corpus evidences. Two genuine BOUNDARY cases emerge: (1) G4982 sōzō warrants BOUNDARY on homonymic/polysemic grounds — its physical-rescue sense (27 verses: drowning, illness, mortal danger) is functionally distinct from its eschatological sense, and the physical verses' inner-being content (fear, urgent physical faith-cry) may warrant sense-split treatment or sub-group-by-sense design, though no verse is entirely disconnected from M38's characteristic; (2) H3722B ka.phar warrants BOUNDARY on supportive/qualifying grounds — its 77-verse corpus is dominated by priestly-mediation machinery (priest mediating appears in the majority of verses) which is a relational-act register rather than an inner-being state at the receiving end; the priestly-mediation axis is so pervasive that the term may function as a qualifying/instrumental register rather than the primary characteristic itself, though its atonement outcomes (conscience cleansed, guilt removed, forgiven) do map to M38. The analyst notes that the 'priest mediating' sub-axis in ka.phar is the cluster's most significant structural tension: it describes the agent and mechanism of atonement rather than the inner-being state of the atoned-for person, and researcher decision is needed on whether this instrumentality belongs in M38 or in a ministry/mediation cluster. The gift terms (G1431, G1434) are thin in verse count but their meanings consistently narrate the reception of salvation-relevant divine gift; they STAY with a cross-register flag toward M39 Blessing. All salvation-register terms (G4990, G4991, G4992, H3444, H6299) STAY with various cross-register adjacencies noted.

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M39 Blessing — grace-as-gift vocabulary (Act 10:45, Rom 5:15, 2Cor 9:15) overlaps with M39's blessing/favour/grace characteristic; also cross-register with M31 Faith in verses where gift-reception is conditioned on faith exercised (Act 11:17, Rom 3:25 via hilastērios context, Eph 2:8 adjacent)
verdicts.rationale: All 9 IB verses name salvation-relevant divine gift as the core meaning: Joh 4:10 ('the gift of God as living water — inner-being state is potential transformation from spiritual thirst'); Rom 5:15 ('the free gift of grace through Christ far exceeds the trespass of Adam — inner-being effect is overflowing gift received against backdrop of condemnation'); Rom 5:17 ('those who receive the abundance of grace and the gift of righteousness will reign in life — inner-being transforms from death-dominated to life-reigning through received righteousness'). The term's meaning corpus consistently narrates the human person at the receiving end of God's redemptive action — exactly M38's characteristic. The cross-register adjacency to M39 is real (grace-gift vocabulary) but every verse is simultaneously evidence of salvation-relevant content, so no transfer is warranted.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: dōrea names an unrecognized divine gift whose reception requires recognizing the giver; the inner-being state is potential transformation from spiritual thirst to living sustenance
the free gift of grace through Christ far exceeds the trespass of Adam — inner-being effect is overflowing gift received against the backdrop of condemnation
the Spirit as the salvation-bearing divine gift given upon repentance, linking the inner turning of will with the reception of divine presence
those who once tasted the heavenly gift and shared in the Spirit have received real salvation-experience — enlightenment and Spirit-participation are the gift's inner content

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M39 Blessing — the single verse (Rom 5:16) is structurally identical to the dōrea register and its grace-as-gift vocabulary is adjacent to M39
verdicts.rationale: The single IB verse (Rom 5:16) is unambiguous: 'unlike the single trespass that brought condemnation, the free gift following many trespasses brings justification — the inner-being state moves from guilt-accumulated to justified-by-gift.' This directly maps to M38's characteristic of the human person at the receiving end of God's redemptive action, with condemnation overcome and justification received. The one-verse corpus does not permit elaborate analysis, but it is free of ambiguity: the term sits squarely in M38.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: the free gift following many trespasses brings justification — the inner-being state moves from guilt-accumulated to justified-by-gift, with the gift's magnitude matching the extent of sin

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M02 Anger — both verses evidence wrath-facing as the structural opposite that propitiation addresses (Luk 18:13: 'wrath facing'; Heb 2:17: mediator enables effective atonement against guilt); also cross-register with M09 Humility in Luk 18:13 (the inner-being crushed, standing at distance, confessing unworthiness)
verdicts.rationale: Both IB verses place the human inner being at the receiving end of propitiatory mercy: Luk 18:13 ('the tax collector's prayer — be merciful to me, a sinner — inner being is crushed, sin-aware, and wholly dependent on divine mercy') and Heb 2:17 ('Christ made propitiation for the people's sins; the inner being's guilt is addressed by a sympathetic mediator'). The meaning corpus is small (2 verses) but entirely consistent with M38's characteristic. No verse lacks M38 relationship, so no transfer. The cross-register with M02 Anger is structural-opposite (wrath being averted) and with M09 Humility (the penitent inner posture).

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: hilaskomai is a plea for propitiatory mercy from one who stands at distance, confessing unworthiness; the inner being is crushed, sin-aware, and wholly dependent on divine mercy

Christ became fully human to serve as a merciful high priest and make propitiation for the people's sins; the inner being's guilt is addressed by a sympathetic mediator whose solidarity enables effective atonement

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M02 Anger — wrath-averted is the structural opposite present in both verses (1Jo 2:2: 'wrath-averting, sin-covering provision'; 1Jo 4:10: 'propitiation; wrath averted'); cross-register with M05 Love — 1Jo 4:10 grounds propitiation in divine love as its source
verdicts.rationale: Both IB verses present hilasmos as the objective sin-covering provision that places the human inner being in the atoned-for state: 1Jo 2:2 ('Christ himself is the propitiation — not merely for a limited group but for the whole world, making atonement universally available to the sin-laden') and 1Jo 4:10 ('God's prior love is the source of propitiation; the inner-being receives atonement as pure gift from divine love'). The corpus is squarely M38. The cross-register with M02 Anger is through the structural-opposite wrath-averted axis, and with M05 Love through the divine-love-as-source axis in 1Jo 4:10.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: Christ himself is the propitiation — the wrath-averting, sin-covering provision — making atonement universally available to the sin-laden
God's prior love, not human initiative, is the source of propitiation; the inner-being receives atonement as pure gift from divine love

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M31 Faith — Rom 3:25 names faith as the inner faculty through which propitiation is received ('received by faith'); cross-register with M02 Anger — wrath averted and former sins passed over is the structural-opposite axis
verdicts.rationale: The single IB verse (Rom 3:25) presents hilastērios as the foundational atonement mechanism: 'Christ is set forth by God as the propitiation through his blood, received by faith — the inner-being effect is that wrath is averted and former sins are passed over, establishing the foundational mechanism of atonement.' This maps directly to M38's characteristic. The cross-register with M31 Faith is evident because faith is the named inner-faculty of reception, but the verse's primary content is the atoning provision itself.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: Christ is set forth by God as the propitiation through his blood, received by faith — the inner-being effect is that wrath is averted and former sins are passed over, establishing the foundational mechanism of atonement

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M12 Purity/Holiness — Heb 8:12 describes conscience cleared and sins no longer remembered, which is adjacent to the purity/cleansing register; cross-register with M02 Anger — wrath averted as the structural opposite
verdicts.rationale: The single IB verse (Heb 8:12) maps to M38's characteristic: 'God promises to be merciful toward iniquities and to remember sins no more; the inner being is freed from the burden of remembered guilt — propitiation produces total forgiveness and conscience cleared.' The inner-being state described (forgiven, guilt released, conscience cleansed, wrath averted) is precisely what M38 names as the outcome of divine redemptive action. No ambiguity warrants BOUNDARY; no verse is disconnected from M38.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: God promises to be merciful toward iniquities and to remember sins no more; the inner being is freed from the burden of remembered guilt — propitiation produces total forgiveness and conscience cleared

verdicts.rationale: The 93-verse corpus divides with textual clarity into at least three discriminable senses that do not share the same inner-being content: (1) eschatological/spiritual salvation (e.g., Mat 1:21: 'save his people from their sins — spiritual rescue targeting the inner person's bondage to sin'; Luk 7:50: 'your faith has saved you — spiritual/eschatological salvation through faith, including forgiveness, with peace as the inner-being state'; Mar 16:16: 'belief and baptism yield eschatological salvation; unbelief yields condemnation'); (2) physical rescue from mortal danger (Mat 8:25: 'Save us in mortal danger of drowning — physical rescue, with the inner being gripped by terror and crying out in urgent faith'; Mat 14:30: 'Peter sinking cries Lord save me — physical rescue, inner being moving from bold venture to panic'; Act 27:20: 'all hope of physical rescue was abandoned — sōzō in its physical/temporal rescue sense, inner state being despair and lost hope'); (3) healing with spiritual overtone (Mat 9:22: 'your faith has made you well — healing with spiritual resonance, with faith as the inner-being faculty that activates the saving act'; Mar 5:34: 'bridges physical restoration and spiritual wholeness, with faith named as the inner faculty'). The physical-rescue sense (sense 2) is functionally distinct — its inner-being content (terror, panic, despair, urgent physical faith-cry) is not the inner-being state of 'being saved from sin/judgment' but the state of being rescued from drowning or storm. While all verses have some relationship with M38 (preventing clean transfer), the polysemic spread is substantial enough that sense-split treatment is needed. Researcher decision required on whether physical-rescue verses remain in M38 as a sub-group or are routed to a physical-danger/deliverance register.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: Jesus named and defined by his mission: to save his people from their sins — the salvation is eschatological/spiritual rescue targeting the inner person's bondage to sin
physical rescue from perishing, with the inner being gripped by terror and crying out in urgent, desperate faith — not the eschatological inner state
sōzō is spiritual/eschatological salvation through faith, including forgiveness, with peace as the inner-being state conferred upon one previously burdened by sin
sōzō in its physical/temporal rescue sense, with the inner state being one of despair and lost hope — no eschatological content present in the verse

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M18 Hope — multiple verses engage the inner faculty of hope as the primary response to the Savior (Phili 3:20: 'the soul's citizenship and expectation are anchored eschatologically'; Tit 2:13: 'hope is the dominant inner faculty, oriented toward future eschatological completion of salvation'; 1Ti 4:10: 'hope actively anchoring the person in God's universal saving will'); cross-register with M22 Praise — Luk 1:47 (Mary's spirit exults in God as Savior) and Rev 7:10 adjacent context
verdicts.rationale: All 17 IB verses consistently name Christ or God as Savior and describe the inner-being state of those who receive salvation from him: Luk 1:47 ('Mary's spirit exults in God as her Savior — the inner being engaged in joyful, worshipful response to divine rescue'); Luk 2:11 ('sōtēr identifies the person of Jesus as the singular source of salvation, grounding hope in a historical event with eschatological scope'); 2Ti 1:10 ('the Savior's appearing abolished death and brought immortality to light — mortality's fear displaced by life revealed'). The term's function is to name the personal source of the M38 characteristic; it belongs squarely in M38. The cross-register with M18 Hope is strong in the eschatological-waiting verses, but hope is the inner-faculty response to the Savior rather than the cluster's primary characteristic.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: Mary's spirit exults in God as her Savior — the inner being (spirit) is engaged in joyful, worshipful response to divine rescue, naming God as the personal source of salvation experienced in the soul
the inner being is oriented in hopeful anticipation toward the Savior returning from heaven, soul's citizenship and expectation anchored eschatologically
the Savior's appearing abolished death and brought immortality to light through the gospel; the inner being is addressed at the deepest level — mortality's fear displaced by life revealed
the Father sent the Son as Savior of the entire world, grounding the inner-being's assurance of rescue in apostolic testimony about Christ's universal saving mission

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M18 Hope — eschatological orientation pervades the corpus (Heb 9:28: 'the inner being is sustained in active, eager anticipation of eschatological completion'; 1Pe 1:5: 'believers are guarded by God's power through faith for a salvation yet to be revealed'); cross-register with M22 Praise — doxological response verses (Rev 7:10: 'the redeemed multitude ascribes salvation entirely to God and the Lamb'; Rev 19:1: 'heaven's multitude ascribes salvation in a hallelujah cry'); cross-register with M11 Repentance — 2Cor 7:10 ('godly grief produces repentance that leads to salvation')
verdicts.rationale: The 42-verse corpus is the core lexical carrier of M38's cluster characteristic. Verse after verse describes the inner-being state of receiving, awaiting, being assured of, or responding with praise to salvation: Rom 1:16 ('the gospel is God's power unto salvation for everyone who believes — faith is the reception point'); Phili 2:12 ('work out your own salvation with reverent seriousness — salvation is a present ongoing state engaging the will, conscience, and deep moral seriousness'); Heb 2:3 ('to neglect so great a salvation is to risk inescapable judgment — conscience and will are culpable'). The term belongs unambiguously in M38. Cross-register adjacencies to M18 Hope and M22 Praise are genuine but subordinate to the salvation-characteristic itself.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: the gospel is God's power unto salvation for everyone who believes — the inner act of faith is the reception point, and salvation is the outcome of that divine power meeting human trust
believers are urged to actively work out their own salvation with reverent seriousness — salvation is a present ongoing state engaging the will, conscience, and deep moral seriousness
the redeemed multitude ascribes salvation entirely to God and the Lamb, expressing corporate joy and praise from those fully delivered — salvation as possession and doxological response
godly grief produces repentance that leads to salvation without regret — salvation is reached through a specific inner journey: godly sorrow, genuine turning, and life-giving outcome

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M18 Hope — Luk 2:30 ('hope fulfilled, producing readiness to depart in peace') and Luk 3:6 ('corporate hope for a salvation that transcends ethnic boundaries') both engage the hope-dimension; cross-register with M23 Strength — Eph 6:17 frames salvation as protective armor in spiritual warfare
verdicts.rationale: All 5 IB verses map to M38's characteristic: Luk 2:30 ('Simeon sees the child and declares his eyes have witnessed God's salvation — producing a readiness to depart in peace as one whose hope is fulfilled'); Eph 6:17 ('the helmet of salvation is worn as protective armor — salvation is an inner reality that shields the mind and identity of the believer'); Tit 2:11 ('God's grace has appeared bringing salvation to all people — the inner being encounters salvation as something externally given by divine grace'). The term names the content of divine saving work as apprehended/received by the human person, squarely in M38.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: sōtērion is salvation made visible and personally witnessed, producing a readiness to depart in peace as one whose hope is fulfilled
salvation is an inner reality that shields the mind and identity of the believer against spiritual assault, a possession worn for defensive protection
God's grace has appeared bringing salvation to all people; the inner being encounters salvation as something externally given by divine grace — an unearned gift now present and available to all
God's salvation has been sent to the Gentiles who will listen — the receptive inner posture of hearing contrasts with those who rejected it

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M18 Hope — the corpus is heavily weighted toward hope-sustained as the inner posture while awaiting salvation (Gen 49:18: 'soul actively waits in expectant longing for God's salvation'; Isa 25:9: 'patient waiting vindicated when salvation arrives'; Isa 59:11: 'salvation is far off — inner being groans in unfulfilled longing'); cross-register with M04 Joy — multiple verses name joy as the inner-being response to received salvation (Isa 12:3: 'salvation pictured as a well from which the redeemed draw with joy'; Isa 35:10: 'those ransomed return with everlasting joy')
verdicts.rationale: The 21-verse corpus consistently names the inner-being state of those who receive, await, or respond to divine salvation: Isa 12:2 ('God himself is named as the speaker's salvation, replacing fear with trust and strength — the inner being rests securely in present experienced deliverance'); Isa 25:9 ('salvation transforms the soul from waiting tension to exultant joy'); Isa 49:6 ('God's salvation extended to all nations — inner-being scope is universal inclusion in deliverance'). The cross-register adjacencies to M18 Hope and M04 Joy are strong but the verses' primary content is the salvation-state itself, not hope or joy as independent topics. STAYS without ambiguity.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: God himself is named as the speaker's salvation, replacing fear with trust and strength, so the inner being rests securely in present experienced deliverance
salvation transforms the soul from waiting tension to exultant joy — patient waiting is vindicated when salvation arrives
the soul actively waits in expectant longing for God's salvation, naming the LORD as its only source of deliverance — an inner posture of hope directed entirely toward divine rescue
God's salvation is extended through the servant to all nations to the ends of the earth — the inner-being scope is universal inclusion in deliverance

verdicts.rationale: The 77-verse corpus divides into two distinct registers within the atonement domain: (a) the inner-being state of the atoned-for person (conscience cleansed, guilt removed, forgiven — e.g., Lev 4:20: 'priestly atonement results in the definitive inner-being outcome of forgiveness — guilt is objectively removed and the conscience cleared'; Lev 16:30: 'the Day of Atonement effects comprehensive cleansing of all sins — the inner being is unburdened from the full year's accumulated sin-guilt'; Isa 6:7: 'the burning coal's touch enacts atonement — guilt is removed and sin covered, cleansing the conscience so the prophet stands before God without condemnation'); and (b) the priestly-mediation machinery (the priest's acts, ordination, ritual sequence, Day of Atonement mechanics — e.g., Lev 9:7: 'Aaron commanded to offer atonement for himself first and then for the people — establishing that an uncovered mediator cannot cover others'; Lev 16:17: 'the high priest enters the Holy Place alone to make atonement for himself, his house, and all Israel — his solitary mediation carries the weight of the entire community's standing'; Num 16:47: 'Aaron's swift priestly action with incense makes atonement and arrests the plague — mediated covering'). Register (a) maps cleanly to M38's characteristic. Register (b) describes the instrumental agent and mechanism — the priestly act itself — which is a qualifying/enabling process rather than the inner-being state at the receiving end. The 'priest mediating' keyword dominates approximately 30+ verses of the 77, creating a body of meaning that consistently describes what the priest does rather than what the atoned-for person experiences. This is a valid supportive/qualifying register concern per §6.1.4. Researcher decision is needed: sense-split treatment separating the atoned-for inner state (STAYS in M38) from the priestly-mediation instrumentality (possible route to a ministry/service cluster or sub-group boundary) is the most precise resolution, but this cannot be pre-resolved without researcher direction.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: priestly atonement through the sin offering results in the definitive inner-being outcome of forgiveness — guilt is objectively removed and the conscience cleared before God
the high priest enters the Holy Place alone to make atonement for himself, his house, and all Israel — his solitary mediation carries the weight of the entire community's standing before God
the Day of Atonement effects comprehensive cleansing of all sins before the LORD — the inner-being outcome is total cleanness, the conscience unburdened from the full year's accumulated sin-guilt
Aaron's swift priestly action with incense makes atonement and arrests the plague — the inner-being community standing between wrath and safety through mediated covering

verdicts.cross_register_flag: Primary M38; cross-register relationship with M37 Calling — several verses ground ransom in God's covenantal election and identity-formation (Deu 7:8: 'God's redemption grounded in his love and covenant oath'; 2Sa 7:23: 'Israel is the unique nation God himself went out to redeem, establishing his name'; 1Ch 17:21: 'redemption constitutes the people's identity and his glory'); cross-register with M04 Joy — ransomed-state issues in sustained rejoicing (Isa 35:10, Isa 51:11, Psa 71:23); cross-register with M41 Remembrance — multiple verses engage memory of past ransom as inner-being motivating force (Deu 15:15, Psa 78:42, Mic 6:4)
verdicts.rationale: The 39-verse corpus consistently describes the human person as the recipient of God's ransoming/redemptive act, with the inner-being states of gratitude, trust, joy, identity-formation, and hope as the outcomes: Psa 130:8 ('God will ransom Israel from all its iniquities — a comprehensive eschatological redemption; the soul's deepest hope is total redemption from sin's hold'); Isa 35:10 ('those ransomed by the Lord return with everlasting joy crowning them — ransom transforms inner being from sorrow and sighing to sustained rejoicing'); Job 33:28 ('God's ransom of the soul from the pit results in restored life and light — the inner being moves from death-facing darkness to life and sight'). The term belongs squarely in M38 as the Hebrew parallel to the soteria/sōzō register. Cross-register adjacencies are notable but no verse falls outside M38's characteristic entirely.

evidence_verses.meaning_excerpt: God will ransom Israel from all its iniquities — a comprehensive eschatological redemption that covers every sin; the soul's deepest hope is total redemption from sin's hold
those ransomed by the Lord return with everlasting joy crowning them — ransom transforms inner being from sorrow and sighing to gladness, marking the ransomed state as one of sustained rejoicing
God's ransom of the soul from the pit results in restored life and light; the inner being moves from death-facing darkness to life and sight through divine redemption
God announces ransom from Sheol and redemption from Death itself — the inner being faces ultimate annihilation, and God's saving act reaches to the deepest existential threat possible
